---
title: "Hello, world — a feature tour"
description: "How math, code, and citations render on this blog."
author: "Krimler"
date: "2026-06-07"
categories: [meta, math, ml]
image: ""        # optional: drop a thumbnail.png here for the listing card
---

Welcome. This post exists to show every building block you'll reuse.

## Math

Inline math like $e^{i\pi} + 1 = 0$ works, and so do display equations:

$$
\nabla_\theta \, \mathcal{L}(\theta) = \mathbb{E}_{x \sim \mathcal{D}}\!\left[ \nabla_\theta \, \ell(f_\theta(x), y) \right]
$$ {#eq-gradient}

You can cross-reference it: see @eq-gradient.

## Code

```python
import torch

def attention(q, k, v):
    scores = q @ k.transpose(-2, -1) / k.size(-1) ** 0.5
    return torch.softmax(scores, dim=-1) @ v
```

Readers get a copy button automatically. To point at real code, just link the
repo: [the implementation lives here](https://github.com/krimler).

## Citing arXiv papers

Cite a paper inline like this [@vaswani2017attention] and the reference list is
built automatically at the bottom from `references.bib`. To add a paper: open its
arXiv page → "Export BibTeX citation" → paste the entry into `references.bib`.

## References

::: {#refs}
:::
